description: A lore record in The Ptah Protocol. The world's history book — the accumulated narrative of what has happened. This is what separates the protocol from a game or a social feed. Games have scores. Social feeds have posts. A world has history.

main.description: The Shabaka Stone of the world. It preserves what happened. It cannot be erased. It names who made it. It is the theological text of a world that anyone can read and nobody can alter.

title.description: The name of this piece of lore.

worldReference.description: The AT URI of the world this lore belongs to.

sourceReferences.description: AT URIs of the action and event records this lore was generated from. Lore does not appear from nowhere — it traces back to things that actually happened. This is the most important field in this record.

characters.description: AT URIs of the character records involved in this lore.

timelinePosition.description: Where this sits in the world's chronology. In-world time, era, epoch, cycle — whatever the world uses.

canonicalStatus.description: The canonical standing of this lore within its world.

authorshipRecord.description: Permanent link to the creator. Provenance travels with every piece of history.

contributionType.description: Whether this lore was written by the world originator or a community contributor.
